title: Some properties of optimal functions for sphere packing in dimensions 8 and 24

thm:2.1.summary: Restatement of the Cohn–Elkies LP bound: admissible $f$ with $f(0)=\widehat{f}(0)\ne 0$, $f(x)\le 0$ for $|x|\ge r$, $\widehat{f}(t)\ge 0$ everywhere bounds the sphere packing density in $\mathbb{R}^n$.

conjecture:2.2.summary: Magic-function existence conjecture (Cohn–Elkies Conjecture 7.3): for $n\in\{2,8,24\}$, optimal $f$ in the sense of Theorem 2.1 exists with the specified $r$ values — now a theorem for $n=8$ (Viazovska, 1603.04246) and $n=24$ (CKMRV, 1603.06518).

lem:3.1.summary: Polynomial-times-Gaussian construction: for algebraic numbers $0<r_1<\cdots<r_k$ there is a unique polynomial $q_k$ of degree $4k-1$ such that $f_k(x)=q_k(|x|^2)e^{-\pi|x|^2}$ satisfies the forced-root constraints — the numerical-method ansatz approximating the optimal $f$.

conjecture:4.1.summary: As $k\to\infty$, $f_k$ and $\widehat{f}_k$ converge to limits $f$, $\widehat{f}$ on a neighborhood of $\mathbb{R}$ in $\mathbb{C}$, uniformly on compacta — the numerical-method convergence conjecture.

conjecture:4.2.summary: The limiting $f$ and $\widehat{f}$ have no real roots other than the forced roots — the no-extra-zeros structural prediction.

conjecture:4.3.summary: Explicit closed form for $f(0)/\widehat{f}(0)$ when roots are forced at $E_8$ vector lengths ($0<n<10$) or Leech lengths ($0<n<26$): a rational function of $n$ with poles at the resonant dimensions $10, 14, 18$ (and analogues for Leech).

lem:5.1.summary: Rescaling identity for the optimal $f$: $f'(r_1) = -\frac{n}{N r_1}\widehat{f}(0)$, with $r_1$ the first forced root and $N$ the kissing number — the linear constraint linking the derivative at $r_1$ to the kissing-number normalization.

conjecture:5.2.summary: Quadratic Taylor coefficients of the optimal $f$ (normalized $f(0)=\widehat{f}(0)=1$): $-27/10$ and $-3/2$ for $n=8$; specific rationals (e.g. $-14347/540$) for $n=24$.

conjecture:5.3.summary: Fourth moment $M_f(4)=M_{\widehat{f}}(4)=1/15$ for the $n=8$ optimal $f$, normalized $f(0)=\widehat{f}(0)=1$.

conjecture:6.1.summary: Energy-minimization identity: for $\varphi(x)=e^{-c|x|^2}$ in $\mathbb{R}^n$ ($n=8$ or $24$), the auxiliary $h$ satisfies $\widehat{h}(0) = \frac{2c}{n} E_\psi(\Lambda_n)$ — connecting the magic-function framework to energy minimization, the conjecture later proved as the CKMRV universal-optimality theorem (1902.05438).

conjecture:7.1.summary: Single-root forcing produces Fourier eigenfunctions: as $k\to\infty$ with $n,\varepsilon$ fixed, the suitably-normalized $g^\varepsilon_{n,k}$ converge to an $\varepsilon$-eigenfunction of the Fourier transform vanishing at all radii $\sqrt{2j}$ — the structural prediction behind the interpolation framework of Radchenko–Viazovska.

conjecture:7.2.summary: Closed-form Fourier eigenfunctions: e.g. $g^0_4(x) = \frac{\sin(\pi|x|^2/2)}{\pi|x|^2/2}\,e^{-\pi\sqrt{3}|x|^2/2}$ (up to scaling), and analogous formulas for $n>4$ a multiple of 4.

prop:7.3.summary: The functions in Conjecture 7.2 are Fourier eigenfunctions with the stated eigenvalues — verified by direct calculation using that $e^{-\pi|x|^2\alpha}$'s Fourier transform is computable in closed form for $\Re(\alpha)>0$.

edges.note: Conjecture 2.2 conjectures the existence of magic functions in the sense of Theorem 2.1 for $n\in\{2,8,24\}$.
The convergence conjecture concerns the polynomial-times-Gaussian sequence $f_k$ constructed in Lemma 3.1.
The no-extra-zeros conjecture is a refinement of the convergence conjecture.
The closed-form $f(0)/\widehat{f}(0)$ formula applies to the limiting function of Conjecture 4.1.
The rescaling identity is derived assuming an optimal $f$ exists in the sense of Conjecture 2.2.
The quadratic Taylor coefficients refine the rescaling identity (Lemma 5.1) to second order.
The fourth-moment conjecture extends the Taylor-coefficient pattern.
The energy-minimization conjecture reformulates the magic-function existence in terms of pairwise energy — the formulation later proved by CKMRV as universal optimality.
The convergence-to-eigenfunction conjecture rests on the eigenfunction property verified in Proposition 7.3.
The closed-form eigenfunctions are explicit instances of the general convergence prediction.

LP_foundation.description: The Cohn–Elkies LP bound, restated as the framework these properties live inside.

magic_function_conjecture.description: The central existence conjecture (now a theorem for $n=8,24$).

numerical_method.description: Polynomial-times-Gaussian approximations $f_k$ and the convergence / no-extra-zeros / closed-form conjectures about their limits.

exact_values.description: The rescaling identity for $f'(r_1)$ and the predicted Taylor coefficients and moments of the optimal $f$.

energy_and_eigenfunctions.description: The energy-minimization reformulation (the conjecture later proved as CKMRV universal optimality) and the Fourier-eigenfunction structure (the prediction behind the Radchenko–Viazovska interpolation framework).

CE.title: New upper bounds on sphere packings I (Cohn–Elkies)

V.title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 8 (Viazovska)
